cli.dependencies: zero npm dependencies; relies exclusively on Node built-ins (node:fs, node:test, node:assert/strict)
cli.packaging: single private package exposing bin `wordy` -> src/cli_main.mjs
cli.lint: .eslintrc.json present (root, es2022, no-unused-vars) but eslint is not declared in package.json or package-lock.json

ordering.rationale: Single layer (cli at '.') with no internal package edges, so there is no cross-package dependency ordering to honor. Within the package the module graph is a simple fan-in: cli_main.mjs imports parse_args.mjs, word_count.mjs, and format_table.mjs, which are mutually independent leaves. Dispatch is trivially single-stage; tasks against this repo can be ordered freely.

gaps: No dependencies or devDependencies declared in package.json and package-lock.json packages map is empty — external-dependency signal per layer is absent by construction, not by omission.
ESLint config (.eslintrc.json) exists but eslint itself is not a declared devDependency and no lint script is defined, so the lint toolchain cannot be confirmed from manifests alone.
Only one source module (parse_args, word_count) is covered by tests; format_table.mjs and cli_main.mjs have no test coverage signal.
No build step exists (no build script), so build-ordering signals are inapplicable rather than detected.
